title: Maisie Austin on her unique Territory life

evidence_summary: nteresting and treasured memories of her Territory childhood. Over the years, Maisie Austin has turned her hand to almost everything: retail and dressmaking, insurance and even a tilt at politics. She's an author, historian and celebrant. And anyone involved with basketball in the Northern Territory knows her well, as a champion player, coach, and longtime stalwart of the scene. These days, Maisie is also the CEO of the NT Stolen Generations Aboriginal Corporation. Maisie remembers her childhood in Parap Camp, the ghost stories of her youth, and some of her great joys and challenges over the years. She spoke to the ABC's Miranda Tetlow on Late Lunch. maisie austin late lunch abc darwin miranda tetlow northern territory darwin childhood basketball.

title: The Ghost Songs

evidence_summary: The Ghost Songs A vivid location documentary recorded at two small Northern Territory Aboriginal communities linked by their music, culture and history, and by their country around the Daly River. A vivid location documentary recorded at two small Northern Territory Aboriginal communities linked by their music, culture and history, and by their country around the Daly River. Usually Wadeye, 200 kilometres south-west of Darwin, is represented as a dysfunctional disaster area, but together with Belyuen on the Cox Peninsula it is the focus of a revolutionary musical project which is already yielding a positive cultural outcome. Musicologists Allan Marett and Linda Barwick have been capturing thousands of traditional Indigenous songs in a step forward.
